Text.Main: Now exiting to Cinderfall

--------------------
Reactor Primer
--------------------
This faciliity is called a [$imp]reactor[$/imp].  This reactor, along with others that are significantly larger, are used in the repair and enhancement of several types of items:

* [$npc]Power Plants[$/npc] which power your vehicles
* [$npc]Gizmos[$/npc] such as useable items
* [$npc]Ornaments[$/npc] to decorate and customize your vehicles
* [$npc]Exotic Weapons[$/npc] using strange energies and ammunitions

Press Main' for the main Crafting Primer.
Press 'Structures' for an overview of the crafting structures.
--------------------

--------------------
Fabrication Plant Primer
--------------------
This faciliity is called a [$imp]Fabrication Plant[$/imp].  This fabrication plant, along with others that are significantly larger, are used in the repair and enhancement of several types of items:

* [$npc]Armor[$/npc] which protects your vehicles
* [$npc]Custom Wheels[$/npc] for all types of vehicles
* [$npc]Chassis[$/npc], actual new vehicles
* [$npc]Melee Weapons[$/npc] for that extra edge in collisions
* [$npc]Hazard Kits[$/npc] enabling Wraith mode in your vehicles

Press Main' for the main Crafting Primer.
Press 'Structures' for an overview of the crafting structures.
--------------------

--------------------
Control Shop Primer
--------------------
This crafting structure is called a [$imp]Control Shop[$/imp].  This Control Shop, along with others that are significantly larger, are used in the repair and enhancement of several types of items:

* [$npc]Projectile Weapons[$/npc] for use on vehicles
* [$npc]Energy Weapons[$/npc] for use on vehicles
* [$npc]Melee Weapons[$/npc] for that extra edge in collisions
* [$npc]Exotic Weapons[$/npc] using strange energies and ammunitions
* [$npc]Gizmos[$/npc] such as useable items
* [$npc]Ornaments[$/npc] to decorate and customize your vehicles
* [$npc]Hazard Kits[$/npc] enabling Wraith mode in your vehicles
* [$npc]Exotic Armor[$/npc] to protect your vehicle in rare environments

Press Main' for the main Crafting Primer.
Press 'Structures' for an overview of the crafting structures.
--------------------

--------------------
Repairing Broken Items 
(page 1 of 3)
--------------------
During your travels as a Warrior of the Tribes, you will undoubtedly salvage or find [$imp]Broken Items[$/imp].  To a skilled craftsman, a broken item is not junk.  I can be repaired, if you the right parts.

The first step to repairing a broken item is determining what parts you will need.  Open your inventory and mouse over the item, the 'fix list' - or list of broken parts - for the item will be listed in the information roll-over box.

Parts are divided into two types [$imp]Commodities[$/imp], which are elementary pieces such as plastic or salvaged sheet metal, and [$imp]Components[$/imp], which are more complex mechanisms such as a Drive Train.  Commodities, and occassionally some components, can be salvaged from the people and ruins of the wasteland in the course of combat.  However you will typically only find the lowest quality of each commodity you discover.  

However, low quality commodities can be combined into higher quality commodities or components at the [$imp]Refinery[$/imp].

Press 'Next' for more information.
Press 'Main' to return to the front page
Press 'Structures' for information on Crafting Structures
--------------------

--------------------
Repairing Broken Items 
(page 2 of 3)
--------------------
Once you have collected and refined the commodities you need, you will need to find the appropriate structure to perform the repairs near.  Look at your broken item one more time.  It should show you the crafting structure you need to repair this item.  Simply find that structure in a town and remain nearby.

The broken item's information window should also explain which [$imp]Discipline[$/imp] is required to repair the item.  You cannot repair the item if you have not been trained in the required Discipline.  Basic Training in each discipline can be bought from the appropriate trainer in towns.  Look for discipline trainers near the crafting structures that service those disciplines.  For example, if your broken item requires power plants and a reactor,  you will very likely find the Power Plants trainer near the reactor.

Press 'Next' for more information.
Press 'Prev' to return to the previous page.
Press 'Main' to return to the front page
Press 'Structures' for information on Crafting Structures
--------------------

--------------------
Repairing Broken Items 
(page 3 of 3)
--------------------
If you already have the compoments or commodities needed to repair the item and you possess the necessary discipline at the required skill level to repair the item then you are ready to actually perform repairs.

Take the broken item and it's required parts to the crafting structure.  Once you are in range of the appropriate structure the background color of the broken item in your inventory should change to indicate you can repair this item here.  Right Click on the item to open the [$imp]crafting window[$/imp].

The crafting window will show you a list of the items broken items you can repair at this crafting structure.  Select the desired item from the list.  Now, you should see an image of the item itself in the crafting screen and a display of every component or commodity you will need in a bar beneath that image.  If the images of the components are in [$imp]red[$/imp] then you do not yet possess the pieces you will need to complete repairs.  However if everything is correct you will then be able to press the accept button to begin the repairs.  your actual chance of success for the repairs will be shown next to the accept button.

This concludes the basic crafting primer

Press 'Main' to return to the front page
Press 'Structures' for information on Crafting Structures
--------------------

--------------------
Basic Crafting Primer
--------------------
In the Central Wastelands, crafting is done by salvaging and repairing [$imp]Broken Items[$/imp].  Restoring old or damaged technology is considered an art in and of itself, and master mechanics are often called 'crafters' or 'craftsmen.'  The art of repairing old equipement is often called 'crafting' as well.

Repairing, or crafting, is done at [$imp]Crafting Structures[$/imp] inside towns and other installations here in the Central Wastelands.  This garage is equipped with all three crafting structures.

Press 'Crafting' for information on Repairing Broken Items
Press 'Structures' for information on the Crafting Structures
--------------------

--------------------
Crafting Structres
--------------------
Crafting is done at one of 3 Crafting Structures, the Reactor, the Control Shop, or the Fabrication Plant.

Press 'Fabrication' for more information on the [$imp]Fabrication Plant[$/imp].
Press 'Control' for more information on the [$imp]Control Shop[$/imp].
Press 'Reactor' for more information on the [$imp]Reactor[$/imp].
Press Main' for the main Crafting Primer.
--------------------
The Reactor
The Fabrication Plant
The Control Shop

Oh, hey there [$class].  Did you need something refined?  [$emote]When you say nothing he smiles.[$/emote]  No?  Perhaps you just wanted to learn what refining is all about, then?  All you have to do is ask, I'll be happy to play teacher for a little while.

You can salvage all sorts of material out there in the ruins.  Plastics, glass, metals, even duct tape.  This is good, because we use all this stuff in crafting.  The problem is, the stuff you find in ruins is usually, well, ruined, right?  Usually you need to do a little repair work to the materials themselves to make them workable in your crafting.  We call this spot repair 'refining' because it takes a bunch of these ruined objects to make a functional one.

There are actually four 'levels' or 'tiers' to each commodity out there.  I can explain in further detail if you'd like, or if you'd like I can continue to explain refining itself.

I mentioned there are four 'tiers' to each commodity, so let me explain what that means. Let's use Duct Tape as an example, out there in the wastelands you'll find [$imp]Salvaged[$/imp] Duct Tape, but that stuff is all frayed; you can't really do anything with it.

However, if you take a bunch of Salvaged Duct Tape to a [$npc]refinery chief[$/npc], someone like me, I can 'refine' a bunch of Salvaged Duct Tape into [$imp]Patched[$/imp] Duct Tape which you can use in crafting.  The amount you need to refine up to the next tier depends on the commodity.

The four tiers are:
1) [$imp]Perfect[$/imp]
2) [$imp]Functional[$/imp]
3) [$imp]Patched[$/imp]
4) [$imp]Salvaged[$/imp]

Anyway, like I said, you can refine the Salvaged commodities you find into more useful versions by speaking to a Refinery Chief, like me.

To do this find the Refinery Chief near the refinery and speak with him.  This will open the Refinery Interface.  

The Refinery Interface will show you a list of the things in your inventory that you can refine up to the next quality tier.  To refine one of these, click on the entry in the list and press the Refine Button.  

This service isn't free and the cost will scale depending on the quantity and quality of what you're refining, so you'll have to pay the Refinery Chief some clink to do the job.  Once the transaction is complete, however, you'll see a bar showing you how long the refining will take.  Once it's done the Refinery Chief will put the refined commodities into your inventory.

That's really all there is to it, wanna give it a try?

Greetings.  [$emote]The Armor Trainer seems to be sizing you up, perhaps weighing your apparent skill or worth.[$/emote]

Are you thinking of taking up Armor craft?  Repairing broken armor can be a very profitable line of work, especially for an aspiring warrior.  I can [$imp]train[$/imp] you in the art of Armor craft, for a price.

Crafting, what we call repairing broken items or building them from memory, requires a certain amount of knowledge.  To repair armor, for example, you need to understand how armor functions, how it is installed on vehicles, the shapes of each armor panel you make and many other minute details.  Armor Trainers such as myself can teach you this knowledge, if you can afford to pay our price.  When a trainer teaches you his or her craft, they are teaching you that particlar '[$imp]Discipline[$/imp],' and only up to a certain point.  

You have to build on the knowledge.  for example, your first lesson in Armor Training will give you the capability to reach low skill level in that discipline. To go any further than that first 'cap' would require further lessons.

Would you like to know more?

It's also very important to remember that taking a lesson from a trainer does not give you the experience, it only gives you the knowledge.  For example, your first lesson in Armor crafting will give you a skill level of 1 in Armor crafting.  To advance beyond that you must craft basic armors until you gain the skill level required to purchase your next lesson from an Armor Trainer.

Luckily there are [$imp]Training Kits[$/imp] available for sale that can be assembled by anyone with a basic knowledge of the discipline.  These kits provide some hands-on crafting experience and will advance your knowledge of your chosen discipline - to a limit degree.

So, do you want to be trained in Armor craft?

Are you thinking of taking up Projectile Weapon crafting?  Repairing broken Projectile Weapons can be a very profitable line of work, especially since most of the basic weaponry a new warrior can use can be crafted using the Projectile Weapons discipline.  

[$emote]The trainer smiles and points at you.[$/emote]

I can [$imp]train[$/imp] you in Projectile Weapon crafting, for a price.

[$emote]The Hestia Assistance Daemon floats quietly here, awaiting an access.[$/emote]

This Unit recognizes Citizen [$name], 

for a marginal clink payment, Citizen, I can [$imp]Train[$/imp] you in the craft of repairing Power Plants.

Crafting, what we call repairing broken items or building them from memory, requires a certain amount of basic knowledge. You have to build on the knowledge.  for example, your first lesson in Power Plant Training will give you the capability to reach low skill level in that discipline. To go any further than that first 'cap' would require further lessons.

Would you like to know more?

The Refinery

What sort of item do you need?
